skill.name: allra-database-schema
skill.description: Guidance for Allra database design, JPA entities, QueryDSL repositories, DTO projections, and Spring transaction annotations.
skill.summary: Apply Allra database, JPA, QueryDSL, and transaction standards to Spring backend work.
skill.author: Allra-Fintech

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown documentation and Java examples. No executable scripts, network endpoints, credential handling, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fences, inline code formatting, or Java, Gradle, and XML examples. They do not execute commands and do not create a command injection path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance only, and the referenced regions are fenced examples or inline formatting. No shell command runner, script file, or executable instruction path is present.

low_findings.title: Network Fetch Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported fetch locations are QueryDSL .fetch() terminal methods in Java examples. They do not call a network Fetch API or send data to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code is Java QueryDSL repository sample code. There is no URL, HTTP client, JavaScript Fetch API, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert on the frontmatter description appears to match ordinary text about database schema guidance. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage appears at this location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata for database schema and QueryDSL guidance. I did not find evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are Java entity business methods inside example domain code. They do not inspect the host, enumerate files, read environment values, or collect system metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of illustrative JPA entity methods named activate and addItem. No system command, filesystem inspection, or environment reconnaissance pattern is present.

content.value_statement: Backend teams need consistent rules for JPA entities, QueryDSL repositories, DTO projections, and transactions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to apply Allra database conventions during implementation and review.
actual_capabilities: Guides JPA repository and QueryDSL support interface structure.
Recommends DTO projection patterns using records and QueryDSL annotations.
Explains where QueryDSL queries should live based on the from clause.
Applies Spring transaction annotation rules for read and write service methods.
Provides JPA entity mapping guidance for columns, enums, auditing, and lazy relationships.
limitations: Does not inspect a live database schema or migration history.
Does not generate database-specific SQL performance plans.
Assumes a Spring Boot, JPA, and Hibernate backend context.
Project-specific database rules may override this guidance.

use_cases.title: Create a New Entity
use_cases.description: Use the skill to shape entity fields, constructors, factory methods, auditing columns, and lazy relationships.

use_cases.title: Review QueryDSL Repositories
use_cases.description: Use the skill to check repository placement, support interfaces, DTO projections, and QueryDSL compatibility.

use_cases.title: Standardize Transactions
use_cases.description: Use the skill to decide when service methods need read-only or write transaction annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Add Transaction Annotations
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a service class.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Spring service and add the correct @Transactional annotation to each public method using Allra rules.

prompt_templates.title: Design a JPA Entity
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for a new domain model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a JPA entity for this domain object using Allra entity design rules, including columns, enums, auditing, and relationship loading.

prompt_templates.title: Build QueryDSL Repository Support
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for a custom query.
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement a QueryDSL repository support pattern for this search requirement, and place the query in the repository that matches the from clause.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Database Layer Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across multiple backend files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these repository, service, DTO, and entity changes against Allra database standards. Report each issue and suggest focused fixes.

output_examples.input: A service has read-only methods without transaction annotations.
output_examples.output: The response identifies each read method and recommends read-only transaction annotations. It separates methods that modify state and need write transactions.

output_examples.input: A QueryDSL query starts from User but is placed in an Order repository.
output_examples.output: The response flags the repository placement problem and recommends moving the query to the User repository support layer.

output_examples.input: A new Order entity has eager relationships and missing auditing fields.
output_examples.output: The response recommends lazy relationship loading, explicit join column rules, and standard created and updated timestamp fields.

best_practices: Give the model the relevant entity, repository, DTO, and service files together.
State the target database engine when compatibility or functions matter.
Ask for a review checklist when applying the skill to a pull request.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for database migration review.
Do not request direct repository implementation injection into services.
Do not apply QueryDSL projection rules when the project does not use QueryDSL.

faq.question: Does this skill create database migrations?
faq.answer: No. It guides entity and repository design, but migration files need project-specific review.

faq.question: Can it be used without QueryDSL?
faq.answer: Yes. QueryDSL sections are optional when the project uses another query approach.

faq.question: Does it support PostgreSQL and MySQL?
faq.answer: It gives general compatibility guidance. You should state the database engine for vendor-specific behavior.

faq.question: Will it inspect production data?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation only and does not connect to databases.

faq.question: Can it review transaction boundaries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can recommend read-only and write transaction annotations for Spring service methods.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to Allra?
faq.answer: Yes. It reflects Allra backend conventions, although many rules also fit common Spring projects.
